label: rfecv-stability-elimination
setup: RF impurity FI, cv=3, max_refits=8, 5 beds x 3 seeds, honest 30% holdout AUC, n=900
decision: REJECTED as default (kept opt-in elimination_rule='stability'); default stays 'importance'
reason: Not a replicated majority win: 1 win / 1 tiny loss / 13 ties of 15 (bed,seed) cells. The single decisive win (many_steady seed1: stability 0.8110 n=9 vs importance 0.7169 n=2, +0.094) is real but isolated; the one loss is noise (-0.0002). Mean delta +0.0063 driven entirely by that one cell.
subsumption_evidence: stability_legacy == stability_dispatched bit-identical in every cell, AND importance_legacy == importance_dispatched here (RF impurity FI was steady fold-to-fold so dispatched's value-CV discount did not reorder). Stability adds a DIFFERENT, orthogonal signal (rank-survival frequency at the elimination cut) that dispatched does not capture; it is NOT subsumed, but the added value did not replicate across beds.
cprofile: aggregate_stability 6.26ms->5.32ms/call (p=500x40 runs) after vectorising the per-feature dict build (was 100k Series.__getitem__ calls); remaining cost is shared DataFrame construction, ~once per outer iter, negligible vs CV refits.
biz_value: test_biz_val_wrappers_rfecv_stability.py pins the many_steady-seed1 win: stability holdout AUC >= importance + 0.04 (measured +0.094) AND keeps more steady_* true features.
